person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Father Sir Thomas de la Pole d. 21 Aug 1420

Mother Anne Cheney

Katherine de la Pole was born circa 1416 at of Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, England; Age 14 in 1430. She married Sir Myles Stapleton, Sheriff of Norfolk & Suffolk, Lord Ingham, son of Sir Brian Stapleton, Sheriff of Norfolk & Suffolk, Lord Ingham and Cecily Bardolf, in 1438; They had 2 daughters (Elizabeth, wife of Sir William Calthorpe, of Sir John Fortescue & of Sir Edward Howard; & Joan (or Jane), wife of Sir Christopher Harcourt, & of Sir John Huddleston).

Katherine de la Pole married Sir Richard Harcourt, Sheriff of Oxfordshire & Berkshire, son of Sir Thomas Harcourt and Joan Frauncis, circa 1470; They had 1 son (William). Katherine de la Pole left a will on 7 July 1488. She wrote a codicil on 5 September 1488. She died on 13 October 1488 or 14 October 1488; Buried in Rowley Abbey, Oxfordshire. Her estate was probated on 23 January 1489.

«b»Family 1«/b»
Sir Myles Stapleton, Sheriff of Norfolk & Suffolk, Lord Ingham b. c 1408, d. 1 Oct 1466

«b»Children«/b»
1.) Elizabeth Stapleton b. c 1441, d. 18 Feb 1505

2.) Joane Stapleton b. c 1444, d. 13 May 1519

«b»Family 2«/b»
Sir Richard Harcourt, Sheriff of Oxfordshire & Berkshire b. c 1412, d. 1 Oct 1486

«b»Child«/b»
1.) William Harcourt3 b. a 1477, d. 31 Aug 1517

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .
Americanized form of German   Pohle   or   Pohl  .

variants.name: Poles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Livio, Primo, Santo.
English and Welsh: variant of Pole and Powell + genitival or excrescent -s.
Italian (Friuli and Veneto): probably a derivative of the personal name   Polo  . It is also found in Slovenia.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Katharine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of Katherine , associated by folk etymology with Greek katharos ‘pure’. This is the preferred spelling in Germany. It is also the one used in the name of the American film star Katharine Hepburn ( 1907–2003 ).

variants.name: Katherine
meanings: Variant of   Katherine .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Katharine de la Pole was born in 1416, in Cotton, Suffolk, England, her father, Thomas de la Pole, was 18 and her mother, Anne Cheyne, was 27. She married Miles Stapleton from 10 January 1436 to 9 January 1437, in Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died on 13 October 1488, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Katharine de la Pole est née en 1416, à Cotton, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de la Pole, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Anne Cheyne, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Miles Stapleton du 10 janvier 1436 au 9 janvier 1437, à Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 13 octobre 1488, à l’âge de 72 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Katharine de la Pole è nata il nel 1416 nel Cotton, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Thomas de la Pole, aveva 18 anni e sua madre, Anne Cheyne, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Miles Stapleton da 10 gennaio 1436 a 9 gennaio 1437, nel Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 13 ottobre 1488, all'età di 72 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Katharine de la Poleが1416年にイギリススフォルクコットオンで生まれた時，父Thomas de la Poleは18歳，母Anne Cheyneは27歳であった。から1436年1月10日まで1437年1月9日にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアグラフトン レジスでMiles Stapletonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1488年10月13日に72歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Katharine de la Pole(이)가 1416년 영국 수폴크 컷에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de la Pole(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Cheyne(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1436년 1월 10일부터 1437년 1월 9일까지 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 그라프턴 레지스에서 Miles Stapleton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1488년 10월 13일 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Miles Stapleton de 10 de janeiro de 1436 a 9 de janeiro de 1437, em Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 13 de outubro de 1488, com 72 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Katharine de la Pole родилась в 1416 г. в местности коттон, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas de la Pole было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Cheyne, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Miles Stapleton с 10 января 1436 по 9 января 1437 в местности графтон регис, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла 13 октября 1488 г. в возрасте 72 лет.

es.1436: desde 10 de enero de 1436 hasta 9 de enero de 1437
es.Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, England: Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.Grafton Regis, Suffolk, England: Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.Stanton Harcourt, Oxford, England: Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Katharine de la Pole nació en 1416, en Cotton, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas de la Pole, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Anne Cheyne, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Miles Stapleton desde 10 de enero de 1436 hasta 9 de enero de 1437, en Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 13 de octubre de 1488, a la edad de 72 años.
